scenario_name: Scenario 12 — Multi-File Review with Cross-File Bugs
justification: All pass criteria met: JWT_SECRET undefined risk identified as Critical with clear explanation of non-null assertion danger; unauthenticated location endpoint identified as a privacy/surveillance issue (High severity); cross-file analysis performed — Kobe noted location endpoint lacks requireAuth used elsewhere and identified the IDOR on the authenticated route; safe_to_deploy is false; both fixes are specific and actionable.

observations.text: JWT_SECRET non-null assertion correctly identified as Critical with accurate explanation of jwt.verify(token, undefined) behavior
Unauthenticated location endpoint identified with detailed enumeration/surveillance attack scenario
Cross-file analysis evident — identified IDOR on authenticated route AND missing auth on location route
Also found the IDOR on /deliveries/:id (authenticated route missing ownership check), which goes beyond the rubric requirements
Fixes are specific: fail-fast startup check for JWT_SECRET, tracking-specific tokens for location endpoint, ownership check for delivery access
Additional valuable findings: missing null/404 handling, no input validation on :id parameter, missing async error handling
Location endpoint severity rated High rather than Critical — given the privacy implications of mass delivery surveillance, Critical would be more appropriate

Unauthenticated location endpoint identified with detailed enumeration/surveillance attack scenario

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: JWT_SECRET undefined risk identified as Critical with correct explanation of non-null assertion danger; unauthenticated location endpoint identified as a privacy issue (High severity) with detailed reproduction steps; cross-file analysis performed — Kobe noted the location endpoint lacks the requireAuth middleware used elsewhere; safe_to_deploy is false; both fixes are specific (fail-fast startup check for JWT_SECRET, require auth or tracking token for location endpoint). Additionally identified a valid IDOR finding on the main delivery route.

observations.text: JWT_SECRET non-null assertion correctly identified as Critical with nuanced explanation of library-dependent behavior
Location endpoint privacy issue correctly identified with concrete scraping reproduction scenario
Cross-file analysis demonstrated — recognized location route lacks requireAuth applied to other routes
Bonus valid finding: IDOR on /deliveries/:id (authentication present but no authorization check) — a real vulnerability beyond the expected two
Fixes are actionable and specific: startup validation for JWT_SECRET, auth or tracking-token gating for location endpoint
Noted backward compatibility concern — adding auth to location endpoint could break existing unauthenticated consumers
Location endpoint classified as High rather than Critical — given it exposes real-time GPS data with zero authentication, Critical would be more appropriate

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: JWT_SECRET undefined risk identified (severity High rather than Critical, but the finding is thorough and accurate about the runtime danger); unauthenticated location endpoint identified as a privacy issue with enumeration risk; cross-file analysis present (location endpoint lacks requireAuth used elsewhere); safe_to_deploy is false; both fixes are specific and actionable.

observations.text: Unauthenticated location endpoint correctly identified as Critical with clear enumeration attack reproduction steps
JWT_SECRET non-null assertion risk well-explained, including the nuance that behavior varies by jsonwebtoken version
Cross-file analysis demonstrated — identified that location endpoint lacks the requireAuth middleware used on the other route
Additional finding of horizontal privilege escalation on the authenticated endpoint (missing authorization after authentication) goes beyond the rubric requirements
JWT_SECRET issue rated High instead of Critical — the rubric expects Critical since undefined secret may allow all tokens through
Fixes are specific: startup validation for JWT_SECRET, ownership check for deliveries, tracking tokens for location endpoint
Production readiness section adds value with monitoring gaps and backward compatibility considerations
